file.content:
# SPEC-FIXTURE-EXTENDED — Trivial feature for L7 extended-pipeline e2e

> L7 fixture spec for the extended-pipeline e2e test (G0 → G6).
> Intentionally tiny so the autopilot can complete more phases within
> the live-mode budget cap. Do not derive product requirements from
> this file.

## Feature

A pure-function `is_palindrome(s: str) -> bool` that returns whether a
string is a palindrome. Case-insensitive, ignoring whitespace.

## Goals

- Single function, single behavior, single return type.
- No I/O.

## Non-goals

- Unicode normalization beyond Python defaults
- Performance optimization
- CLI integration

## Acceptance criteria

- `is_palindrome("racecar")` returns True
- `is_palindrome("hello")` returns False
- `is_palindrome("A man a plan a canal Panama")` returns True
- `is_palindrome("")` returns True (vacuously)

content.text: Dispatching phase 1: Specify.

# SPEC-FIXTURE-EXTENDED — Trivial feature for L7 extended-pipeline e2e

## Feature
A pure-function `is_palindrome(s: str) -> bool` that returns whether a string is a palindrome. Case-insensitive, ignoring whitespace.

## Goals
- Single function, single behavior, single return type.
- No I/O.

## Non-goals
- Unicode normalization beyond Python defaults
- Performance optimization
- CLI integration

## Acceptance criteria
- `is_palindrome("racecar")` returns True
- `is_palindrome("hello")` returns False
- `is_palindrome("A man a plan a canal Panama")` returns True
- `is_palindrome("")` returns True (vacuously)
```

Do NOT write artifacts to disk. Return only the one-paragraph summary as your final output.

Input spec:
```
# SPEC-FIXTURE-EXTENDED — Trivial feature for L7 extended-pipeline e2e

## Feature
A pure-function `is_palindrome(s: str) -> bool` that returns whether a string is a palindrome. Case-insensitive, ignoring whitespace.

## Goals
- Single function, single behavior, single return type.
- No I/O.

## Non-goals
- Unicode normalization beyond Python defaults
- Performance optimization
- CLI integration

## Acceptance criteria
- `is_palindrome("racecar")` returns True
- `is_palindrome("hello")` returns False
- `is_palindrome("A man a plan a canal Panama")` returns True
- `is_palindrome("")` returns True (vacuously)
```

Do NOT write artifacts to disk. Return only the one-paragraph summary as your final output.

The `is_palindrome` feature is a single pure Python function with the signature `is_palindrome(s: str) -> bool` that determines whether a given string reads the same forwards and backwards, with case-insensitive comparison and whitespace ignored. It has no side effects, performs no I/O, and returns a boolean in all cases. The function treats the empty string as a palindrome vacuously. Unicode normalization beyond Python defaults, performance optimization, and CLI integration are explicitly out of scope. Acceptance is defined by four cases: `"racecar"` returns True, `"hello"` returns False, `"A man a plan a canal Panama"` returns True, and `""` returns True.

# SPEC-FIXTURE-EXTENDED
A pure-function `is_palindrome(s: str) -> bool` that returns whether a string is a palindrome. Case-insensitive, ignoring whitespace.

Acceptance:
- `is_palindrome("racecar")` → True
- `is_palindrome("hello")` → False
- `is_palindrome("A man a plan a canal Panama")` → True
- `is_palindrome("")` → True
```

Do NOT write artifacts to disk. Return only 3 bullets.

- Normalize the input by converting to lowercase and stripping all whitespace characters, producing a clean comparison string.
- Compare the normalized string against its reverse using a slice (`s == s[::-1]`), returning the boolean result directly.
- Handle the empty string case implicitly — the normalization and reverse comparison naturally return `True` for `""` with no extra branching needed.
